title: narcissus hispanicus subsp. confusus
intro: Narcissus hispanicus subsp. confusus, also known as the Spanish bluebell, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Amaryllidaceae family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include the presence of alkaloids and flavonoids, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antiviral effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems and skin conditions, and its extracts have been studied for their potential to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and viruses. Herbal preparations of Narcissus hispanicus subsp. confusus can be made from the flowers, leaves, or bulbs, and are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of illness.
